segments.text: PART 2
I never really planned to be a lifeboat volunteer when I came to live in Northsea.
segments.translation: 当我来到诺斯海生活时，我从未想过要成为一名救生艇志愿者。

segments.text: I'd been working in London as a website designer, but although that was interesting, I didn't like city life.
segments.translation: 我一直在伦敦做网站设计师，虽然工作很有趣，但我不喜欢城市生活。

segments.text: I'd been really keen on boats as a teenager, and I thought if I went to live by the sea, I might be able to pursue that interest a bit more in my free time.
segments.translation: 十几岁的时候，我就非常喜欢船，我想如果我住在海边，也许能在空闲时间更多地追求我的兴趣。

segments.text: Then I found that the Lifeboat Institution was looking for volunteers, so I decided to apply.
segments.translation: 后来我发现救生艇协会正在招募志愿者，于是我决定申请。

segments.text: The Lifeboat Institution building here in Northsea's hard to miss, it's one of the largest in the country.
segments.translation: 北海的救生艇协会大楼很显眼，它是全国最大的救生艇协会之一。

segments.text: It was built 15 years ago with funds provided by a generous member of the public who'd lived here all her life.
segments.translation: 它建于15年前，由一位慷慨的公众成员提供的资金，她在这里生活了一辈子。

segments.text: As the Lifeboat Institution is a charity that relies on that kind of donation, rather than funding provided by the government, that was a huge help to us.
segments.translation: 因为救生艇机构是一个依赖捐赠的慈善机构，而不是政府提供的资金，这对我们帮助很大。

segments.text: When I applied, I had to have a health assessment.
segments.translation: 当我申请的时候，我必须做一个健康评估。

segments.text: The doctors were particularly interested in my vision.
segments.translation: 医生非常关注我的视力。

segments.text: I used to be short-sighted, so I'd had to wear glasses, but I'd had laser eye surgery two years earlier so that was OK.
segments.translation: 我以前是近视眼，所以不得不戴眼镜，但两年前我做了激光眼科手术，所以视力没问题。

segments.text: They gave me tests for colour blindness and they thought I might have a problem there, but it turned out I was OK.
segments.translation: 他们给我做了色盲测试，他们认为我可能有问题，但结果证明我没事。

segments.text: When the coastguard gets an alert, all the volunteers are contacted and rush to the lifeboat station.
segments.translation: 当海岸警卫队收到警报时，所有的志愿者都会得到联系，并迅速赶往救生艇站。

segments.text: Our target's to get there in five minutes, then we try to get the boat off the dock and out to sea in another six to eight minutes.
segments.translation: 我们的目标是在五分钟内赶到那里，然后在六到八分钟内将船驶离码头并驶向大海。

segments.text: Our team's proud that we usually achieve that - the average time across the country's eight and a half minutes.
segments.translation: 我们团队引以为豪的是，我们通常都能做到这一点--全国的平均时间是八分半钟。

segments.text: I've recently qualified as what's called a 'helmsman', which means I have the ultimate responsibility for the lifeboat.
segments.translation: 我最近获得了所谓的 "舵手 "资格，这意味着我对救生艇负有最终责任。

segments.text: I have to check that the equipment we use is in working order - the crew have special life jackets that can support up to four people in the water.
segments.translation: 我必须检查我们使用的设备是否正常工作--船员们都有特制的救生衣，最多可支持四人下水。

segments.text: And it's ultimately my decision whether it's safe to launch the boat.
segments.translation: 最终由我来决定是否安全放行。

segments.text: But it's very rare not to launch it, even in the worst weather.
segments.translation: 但即使在最恶劣的天气下，也很少有不下水的情况。

segments.text: As well as going out on the lifeboat, my work involves other things too.
segments.translation: 除了出海救生，我的工作还包括其他方面。

segments.text: A lot of people underestimate how quickly conditions can change at sea, so I speak to youth groups and sailing clubs in the area about the sorts of problems that sailors and swimmers can have if the weather suddenly gets bad.
segments.translation: 很多人都低估了海上环境变化的速度，因此我向当地的青年团体和帆船俱乐部讲述如果天气突然变坏，水手和游泳者可能会遇到的各种问题。

segments.text: We also have a lot of volunteers who organise activities to raise money for us, and we couldn't manage without them.
segments.translation: 我们还有很多志愿者，他们组织活动为我们筹款，没有他们，我们无法开展工作。

segments.text: The training we get is a continuous process, focusing on technical competence and safe handling techniques, and it's given me the confidence to deal with extreme situations without panicking.
segments.translation: 训练是一个持续的过程，重点是技术能力和安全操作技巧，这让我有信心应对极端情况而不惊慌失措。

segments.text: I was glad I'd done a first aid course before I started, as that's a big help with the casualty care activities we do.
segments.translation: 我很高兴自己在入职前参加了急救课程，因为这对我们开展伤员救护活动大有帮助。

segments.text: We've done a lot on how to deal with ropes and tie knots - that's an essential skill.
segments.translation: 我们还学习了很多如何处理绳索和打绳结--这是一项基本技能。

segments.text: After a year, I did a one-week residential course, led by specialists.
segments.translation: 一年后，我在专家的带领下参加了为期一周的住宿课程。

segments.text: They had a wave-tank where they could create extreme weather conditions - so we could get experience at what to do if the boat turned over in a storm at night, for example.
segments.translation: 他们有一个造波水池，可以创造极端的天气状况，所以我们可以获得经验，例如如果船在夜间翻覆，在暴风雨中做什么。

segments.text: Since I started, I've had to deal with a range of emergency situations.
segments.translation: 自从我开始工作以来，我不得不处理各种紧急情况。

segments.text: But the work's hugely motivating.
segments.translation: 但这项工作给了我极大的动力。

segments.text: It's not just about saving lives - I've learned a lot about the technology involved.
segments.translation: 这不仅仅是关于拯救生命——我已经学到了很多关于相关技术的知识。

segments.text: My background in IT's been useful here, and I can use my expertise to help other volunteers.
segments.translation: 我的 IT 背景在这里很有用，我可以利用我的专业知识来帮助其他志愿者。

segments.text: They're a great group - we're like a family really, which helps when you're dragging yourself out of bed on a cold stormy night.
segments.translation: 他们是一个很棒的团队--我们就像一个大家庭，当你在寒冷的暴风雨之夜从床上爬起来时，这对你很有帮助。

segments.text: But actually, it's the colder months that can be the most rewarding time.
segments.translation: 但实际上，寒冷的月份才是最有意义的时候。

segments.text: That's when the incidents tend to be more serious, and you realise that you can make a huge difference to the outcome.
segments.translation: 这时候的事故往往更加严重，你会意识到你可以对结果产生巨大的影响。

segments.text: So if any of you listeners are interested. . . .
segments.translation: 所以，如果各位听众有兴趣. . . .
